authors: Liu, Slotine & Barabási
input_type: Network STRUCTURE (directed weighted graph); assumes linear time-invariant (LTI) dynamics on the topology
output_type: Minimum set of driver nodes that render the LTI system structurally controllable (steerable to any state in principle)
limitation: Assumes idealized LTI dynamics and full continuous actuation; 'controllable in principle' says nothing about whether a target is reachable with the discrete loss/gain-of-function perturbations actually available
one_line_delta: Our controllability question is concrete and data-grounded — is this measured target inside the cone of achievable measured perturbation combinations? — answered with a certificate, versus an abstract LTI structural-controllability count.

authors: Gao, Liu, Slotine & Barabási
input_type: Network STRUCTURE (directed graph); LTI dynamics assumed; a specified subset of target nodes
output_type: Minimum driver-node set required to control a chosen SUBSET (target set) of nodes, rather than the whole network
limitation: Still topology + LTI based; 'target set controllable in principle' does not establish achievability of a measured transition with available perturbations, nor certify infeasibility
one_line_delta: We target a measured state vector and test membership in a cone of measured effect generators with a Farkas certificate, instead of counting drivers for structural target-controllability of a node subset under LTI assumptions.

authors: Mochizuki, Fiedler et al.
input_type: Network STRUCTURE (directed regulatory graph); dynamics assumed to be a general ODE system consistent with the topology
output_type: Feedback vertex set — a minimal set of 'determining nodes' whose trajectories uniquely determine the dynamics of the whole network (and thus control/monitor its attractors)
limitation: Purely structural and dynamics-agnostic; identifies determining nodes but neither uses measured effects nor certifies whether a desired transition is achievable
one_line_delta: We replace topology-derived determining nodes with measured effect generators and deliver a convex feasibility/infeasibility certificate for a concrete target transition rather than a structural determination result.

authors: Zañudo & Albert
input_type: Hand-built/curated discrete (Boolean) network model of regulatory logic
output_type: Attractor/steady-state control set — node interventions (fix ON/OFF) that drive the system to a target attractor
limitation: Requires a correct hand-built Boolean model; controls modeled logic, not measured perturbation effects, and offers no provable-infeasibility certificate for a target transition
one_line_delta: We combine MEASURED perturbation effect vectors (not a hand-built Boolean model) into a convex cone and return a Farkas/NNLS feasibility certificate — including a proof that a target lies outside the reachable set — rather than searching a modeled attractor landscape.

authors: Zañudo, Yang & Albert
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
input_type: Network STRUCTURE (directed interaction graph/topology) with nonlinear dynamics assumed
output_type: Driver/control node set (feedback vertex set + source nodes) that guarantees convergence to any of the system's natural dynamical attractors
limitation: Steers only to attractors the model already possesses and needs the true interaction topology; cannot certify reachability/infeasibility of an arbitrary measured target transition
one_line_delta: Our target is an arbitrary measured differential-expression transition tested against a cone of real effect vectors with a numeric feasibility certificate, not a topology-based guarantee of reaching some pre-existing model attractor.

method: Control kernel of biomolecular regulatory networks
authors: Kim, Park & Cho
input_type: Hand-built/curated Boolean (logical) regulatory network model
output_type: Control kernel — a minimal set of nodes whose fixed states determine (control) the network's steady state / cell fate
limitation: Depends on a correct hand-built Boolean model and controls modeled logic rather than measured effects; no feasibility/infeasibility certificate for an arbitrary target
one_line_delta: We derive our minimal intervention set from measured effect vectors via sparse NNLS and back it with a convex feasibility certificate, rather than from the logic of a hand-built Boolean model.

method: Boolean relative dynamic stability of cell states
authors: Joo, Zhou, Huang & Cho
input_type: Hand-built/curated Boolean network model of cell-fate regulation
output_type: Relative dynamic stability of cell states (attractor stability / transition propensities), used to prioritize control interventions between states
limitation: Depends on a hand-built Boolean model and reports stability/propensity rather than achievability; no certificate for a measured target transition
one_line_delta: Joo et al. rank Boolean-model state stability; we test measured reachability of a specific transition and certify feasibility or prove it lies outside the achievable cone.

authors: Marazzi, Shah et al.
input_type: Inferred or curated network model + steady-state (RNA/omics) values; simulates dynamics via a signal-flow (SFA-style) propagation
output_type: Ranked set of control targets (nodes to perturb) predicted to steer the system from an initial toward a desired attractor/steady state
limitation: Prediction/ranking pipeline over an inferred network with simulated dynamics; no measured-effect grounding and no certificate that the target is (or is not) reachable
one_line_delta: We answer reachability with a convex program over measured effect vectors and emit a certificate, whereas NETISCE simulates an inferred network and ranks candidate control sets without any feasibility guarantee.

authors: Rukhlenko, Halász, Kholodenko et al.
input_type: Data-derived: builds a mechanistic signaling/regulatory model and a 'state-transition' vector (STV) from omics + a data-defined cell-state separating surface (SVM)
output_type: Predicted perturbations / driver signaling nodes that move cells along the state-transition vector between defined cell states
limitation: Relies on a fitted mechanistic model and a linear state-transition direction; no convex reachability certificate and no proof when a target is unattainable from available perturbations
one_line_delta: cSTAR fits a mechanistic model and predicts drivers along a state-transition vector; we instead certify whether the transition lies in the cone spanned by MEASURED perturbation effects, returning a provable verdict and an activation certificate when it does not.

authors: Ronquist, Patterson, Rajapakse et al.
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
input_type: Data-derived linear time-invariant (LTI) model built from time-series and Hi-C / genome-architecture data
output_type: Control input (set/combination of genes) computed via control theory to drive the LTI cell-state model toward a target expression state
limitation: Reachability holds only for the fitted linear surrogate; assumes LTI dynamics and full actuation, so it cannot certify achievability with the discrete real perturbations nor prove infeasibility
one_line_delta: We avoid fitting a dynamical (LTI) surrogate: reachability is decided directly on measured perturbation effect vectors as a convex-cone membership test with a Farkas certificate.

authors: Wytock & Motter
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
input_type: Learned functional gene-network / expression model (transfer learning across cell types) from transcriptomic data
output_type: Predicted transcription-factor perturbations / control interventions for direct cell reprogramming
limitation: Learned-model prediction of drivers; does not test convex reachability of a specific target nor certify infeasibility from available perturbations
one_line_delta: Where Wytock & Motter learn a functional network to predict reprogramming TFs, we test a target transition for membership in a cone of measured effects and return a provable feasibility/infeasibility certificate.

authors: Rackham et al. (Mogrify)
input_type: Inferred regulatory-influence network + gene-expression signatures (e.g., FANTOM5) across cell/tissue types
output_type: Ranked set of transcription factors predicted to convert a source cell type into a target cell type
limitation: Ranks TFs from an inferred network + expression atlas; no measured-perturbation cone and no certificate of whether a target conversion is achievable or provably not
one_line_delta: Mogrify ranks candidate TF cocktails from an inferred influence network; we decide reachability of the target state from MEASURED perturbation effects and, when unreachable, name the genes that must be activated via a Farkas certificate.

authors: Cahan et al. (CellNet)
input_type: Inferred gene-regulatory networks (GRNs) from large expression compendia + a cell-type classifier
output_type: Cell-identity/GRN status ASSESSMENT of engineered cells plus prioritized transcriptional regulators to improve a conversion
limitation: Assesses identity and recommends regulators from inferred GRNs; does not test convex reachability of a target transition or certify infeasibility
one_line_delta: CellNet grades cell identity against inferred GRNs and suggests regulators; we instead give a data-grounded yes/no reachability verdict for the transition with a convex certificate and an explicit activation set.

authors: Kamimoto et al.
input_type: Inferred GRN (base GRN from scATAC-seq/motif scan, pruned by scRNA-seq regression) + scRNA-seq.
output_type: Predicted cell-identity/state shift from in-silico TF knockout or overexpression: perturbation signal propagated through the linear GRN model and projected as a transition vector onto a differentiation/pseudotime field.
limitation: Predicts a response on an INFERRED network rather than measuring it; can only perturb TFs present in the base GRN; gives a predicted shift, not a data-grounded reachable/not-reachable verdict or certificate; prediction robustness has been questioned.
one_line_delta: We assemble MEASURED perturbation effect vectors as convex-cone generators and return a falsifiable reachable/not-reachable verdict with a Farkas activation certificate, instead of propagating a perturbation through an inferred network to predict a directional shift.

authors: Wang et al.
input_type: Inferred context-specific/dynamic GRN from single-cell multiomics (scRNA + scATAC) with TF footprinting, resolved along a developmental trajectory.
output_type: Reconstructed dynamic GRN plus in-silico perturbation / regulatory-driver analysis: predicted network-level effect and TF influence/logFC across cell states.
limitation: Outputs are bounded by GRN-inference accuracy and describe predicted influence/effect, not whether a target transition is achievable; no certificate.
one_line_delta: We reconstruct no network at all — we test whether a target transition lies in the cone spanned by real measured perturbation signatures, yielding a certificate rather than a dynamic-network prediction.

authors: Aibar et al.
input_type: scRNA-seq expression; co-expression modules (GENIE3/GRNBoost) refined by motif enrichment (cisTarget) into regulons.
output_type: Regulons (TF + target gene sets) and per-cell regulon activity (AUCell) — a TF-activity / cell-state characterization. It is a network-inference + activity-scoring layer, not an in-silico perturbation simulator.
limitation: Infers regulatory activity but does not predict perturbation outcomes or achievability; it is the foundational inference layer that downstream in-silico-perturbation tools depend on (inheriting its inference error).
one_line_delta: SCENIC scores which regulons are active; we invert the question — given measured perturbation effects, we certify whether a desired state change is reachable and, if not, which genes must be activated.

authors: Bravo Gonzalez-Blas et al.
input_type: Single-cell multiome (scRNA + scATAC / 10x Multiome); enhancer-driven GRNs (eRegulons linking TF -> enhancer -> gene) via motif + region-to-gene modeling.
output_type: Enhancer-GRN (eRegulons) with TF/enhancer/gene activity; supports in-silico TF perturbation simulation over the inferred enhancer network.
limitation: Depends on a noisy enhancer-GRN inference step (motif scan + multiome region-to-gene); yields predicted effects/rankings, not a data-grounded achievability certificate.
one_line_delta: We bypass enhancer/GRN inference and its errors, using measured effect vectors directly to produce a provable reachability verdict with a certificate.

authors: Xu et al.
input_type: Enhancer activity (H3K27ac/ATAC) + expression to build cell-type-specific TF-gene networks for a source and a target cell state.
output_type: Ranked key/influential TFs for a specified cell-fate transition, scored from the differential (source vs target) regulatory network 'influence' score.
limitation: Produces a prioritized TF list from an inferred differential network, not a measured-effect reachability verdict; no certificate of achievability or infeasibility.
one_line_delta: ANANSE ranks candidate TF drivers for a transition; we instead certify whether the transition is achievable at all from the available measured perturbations, and via Farkas name the genes that must be activated if it is not.

authors: Osorio et al.
input_type: A single-cell GRN inferred from one scRNA-seq sample (tensor-decomposition-denoised network).
output_type: Virtual knockout: the gene's row is zeroed in the inferred network, the network is re-embedded and manifold-aligned to the wild-type, and a distance test returns the genes predicted to be differentially regulated after the in-silico deletion.
limitation: Loss-of-function only, on a single inferred network; predicts a KO's gene-level effect, not whether a target CELL-STATE transition is reachable, and gives no certificate or activation prescription.
one_line_delta: scTenifoldKnk predicts which genes change after a virtual KO on an inferred network; we test reachability of a whole target transition from measured effects and, via Farkas, prescribe the required activations.

authors: Pratapa et al.
input_type: scRNA-seq (curated real datasets plus synthetic/simulated datasets with known ground-truth networks); evaluates 12 GRN-inference algorithms.
output_type: Benchmark evaluation: accuracy and stability rankings of GRN-inference methods (AUPRC/AUROC vs ground truth, early-precision, robustness).
limitation: Documents that GRN inference is itself error-prone and strongly method-dependent — the exact inference step that every in-silico-perturbation method in this family relies on — and provides no perturbation or achievability output of its own.
one_line_delta: BEELINE quantifies how unreliable GRN inference is; our method sidesteps inference entirely by using measured perturbation effects, so those accuracy caveats never propagate into our verdict.

authors: Wang et al.
input_type: A cell-lineage-specified GRN (inferred from scRNA-seq) analyzed with a graph neural network plus network control theory.
output_type: Ranked driver regulators of cell-fate decisions and gene-influence scores, with a minimal driver (control) node set derived from network controllability.
limitation: Control-theoretic drivers are computed on an INFERRED network and yield a ranked/critical set, not a measured-effect achievability certificate or a data-grounded gain-of-function prescription.
one_line_delta: CEFCON applies control theory to an inferred network to rank drivers; we apply a convex feasibility test to cones of MEASURED effects, giving a provable verdict plus a Farkas activation certificate rather than a driver ranking.

authors: Cannoodt et al.
input_type: A user-defined/backbone mechanistic gene-regulatory network model (thermodynamic + stochastic-simulation kinetics) — it generates synthetic data rather than consuming real screens.
output_type: Synthetic single-cell datasets with a known ground-truth GRN, trajectories, and optional simulated knockdown/perturbation data for benchmarking downstream methods.
limitation: Produces SIMULATED data from an assumed network; the 'ground truth' is model-defined rather than measured, and it offers no achievability verdict or certificate for a real target transition.
one_line_delta: dyngen fabricates synthetic perturbation data from an assumed network for benchmarking; we operate on REAL measured perturbation screens and return a certified achievability verdict for a real target transition.

authors: Roohani et al.
input_type: scRNA-seq Perturb-seq (measured perturbations) + a prior gene-gene knowledge graph (GO/co-expression) as inductive bias
output_type: Predicted post-perturbation expression profile (differential response) for unseen single- and two-gene genetic perturbations
limitation: Predicts the response to a chosen perturbation, not whether a desired target state is achievable; returns a plausible answer for any query even when the target lies outside what the measured perturbations can reach, and never certifies impossibility
one_line_delta: We pose the inverse of GEARS: instead of predicting a perturbation's response, we test whether a target transition lies in the convex cone spanned by measured effect vectors, and when it does not we emit a Farkas certificate naming genes to activate.

authors: Lotfollahi et al.
input_type: Single-cell expression with perturbation/covariate labels (drug, dose, genetic, cell type) — measured screen data
output_type: Predicted (counterfactual) expression response for new combinations of perturbations/doses/covariates, plus interpretable additive latent embeddings
limitation: Produces a predicted response for any composition under a latent-additivity assumption; gives no data-grounded verdict on whether a target state is reachable and no certificate when it is not
one_line_delta: CPA composes learned latent perturbation vectors to predict a response; we compose measured effect vectors under a nonnegativity constraint to decide reachability and certify infeasibility.

authors: Hetzel et al.
input_type: Single-cell expression + drug chemical structure (molecular encoder) + dose
output_type: Predicted single-cell transcriptional response to novel (unseen) drug molecules at given doses
limitation: Predicts a drug's response signature but cannot say whether a desired cell state is reachable by the available perturbations, and offers no infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: chemCPA predicts responses to unseen drugs; we do not predict a response at all but certify whether a target state is reachable by combining measured genetic effect vectors.

authors: Lotfollahi et al.
input_type: Single-cell expression + condition labels; VAE latent space with vector arithmetic
output_type: Predicted expression of an unseen cell type/species in a perturbed condition via a learned latent delta (out-of-distribution response prediction)
limitation: Adds one learned latent delta to predict a stimulated state; provides no verdict on reachability of an arbitrary target and no certificate of impossibility
one_line_delta: scGen adds a single learned latent delta to predict a stimulated state; we test membership of a target delta in a convex cone of many measured deltas and certify when it is outside.

authors: Piran et al.
input_type: Single-cell expression + known attributes (ordered and categorical labels); disentangled representation learning
output_type: Predicted (generative) expression for unseen attribute combinations — e.g., unseen dose/perturbation/cell-state combinations
limitation: Generates expression for unseen attribute combinations but does not decide whether a target combination of states is reachable, nor certify infeasibility
one_line_delta: Biolord disentangles attributes to generate unseen combinations; we use measured effect vectors to decide and certify whether a target combination of states is reachable.

authors: Cui et al.
input_type: Large-scale single-cell transcriptomes (33M+ cells) for transformer pretraining; fine-tuned per task, including perturbation prediction
output_type: Task-dependent; for perturbation, predicted post-perturbation expression; also cell/gene embeddings, annotation, GRN inference
limitation: A general-purpose pretrained model that outputs responses; provides no data-grounded reachable/not-reachable verdict and no certificate
one_line_delta: scGPT is a pretrained model fine-tuned to output responses; we run a convex feasibility program on measured effect vectors to return a certified reachable/not-reachable verdict.

authors: Theodoris et al.
input_type: ~30M single-cell transcriptomes rank-value encoded for transformer pretraining; enables in-silico perturbation of the learned model
output_type: In-silico deletion/activation predictions of network shifts, disease-driver gene rankings, and embedding-based cell-state classification
limitation: Ranks candidate regulators by perturbing a learned embedding, not measured effect vectors; gives no data-grounded reachability verdict and no infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: Geneformer perturbs a learned embedding to rank candidate regulators; we operate on measured effect vectors and return a provable feasibility certificate rather than a ranking.

authors: Hao et al.
input_type: ~50M single-cell transcriptomes, read-depth-aware transformer pretraining; embeddings feed downstream task models (e.g., GEARS)
output_type: Cell/gene embeddings that boost downstream perturbation-response prediction, drug-response prediction and annotation
limitation: Provides embeddings that improve response prediction but never emits a reachability verdict or infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: scFoundation supplies embeddings that improve response prediction; our oracle bypasses embeddings and tests reachability directly on measured differential-expression vectors with a certificate.

authors: Norman et al.
input_type: Measured combinatorial CRISPRa Perturb-seq (single + double perturbations), rich single-cell phenotypes
output_type: Genetic-interaction manifold and GI classification (synergy, additivity, epistasis, suppression); a low-dimensional map of combined phenotypes (data resource)
limitation: Characterizes combinations that were actually assayed; does not decide whether a new, unassayed target state is reachable, and provides no feasibility program or certificate
one_line_delta: Norman provides the measured combinatorial effect vectors; we build a convex-cone reachability oracle on top of such measured effects to certify achievability of unseen target states.

authors: Schiebinger et al.
input_type: Time-series scRNA-seq (measured); optimal-transport couplings between consecutive timepoints
output_type: Developmental trajectories, ancestor-descendant transport maps, fate probabilities, and putative regulatory drivers of transitions
limitation: Maps how cells do transition over observed time under natural dynamics; it does not tell you whether a transition is achievable by available perturbations, nor certify when it is impossible
one_line_delta: Waddington-OT maps observed temporal transitions via optimal transport; we solve a different convex program — reachability of a target by combining measured perturbation effects — and certify when it is impossible.

authors: Lange et al.
input_type: scRNA-seq with RNA-velocity / pseudotime / similarity kernels combined into a Markov transition matrix
output_type: Initial and terminal state identification, fate probabilities, and driver-gene rankings toward each fate
limitation: Describes spontaneous fate transitions and ranks drivers; it does not decide perturbation-reachability of a target fate nor emit an infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: CellRank estimates fate probabilities along observed dynamics; we test and certify whether a desired fate is reachable by combining measured perturbations, independent of spontaneous dynamics.

method: IterPert (sequential optimal experimental design of perturbation screens)
authors: Huang et al.
input_type: Multi-modal priors (ontologies, pathways, protein/literature embeddings) + measured Perturb-seq feedback in an active-learning loop
output_type: A batch acquisition policy — which perturbations to assay next to learn the screen most label-efficiently
limitation: Optimizes which experiments to run next, not whether a target cell state is reachable from the measured effects; produces no reachability verdict or certificate
one_line_delta: IterPert decides which perturbations to measure next; given the measured effects, we decide whether a target is reachable and certify infeasibility — complementary to, not competing with, experimental design.

authors: Laura Cifuentes-Fontanals, Elisa Tonello et al.
input_type: Boolean network model (logical update rules)
output_type: control-set (node/edge interventions)
limitation: relies on a pre-specified/inferred Boolean logic network rather than directly measured perturbation effects, and provides no dual infeasibility proof or minimal-recipe optimality guarantee when no strategy exists
one_line_delta: Their method searches percolation and trap-space reachability inside a hand-built/inferred Boolean logic model, whereas our oracle uses measured CRISPRi effect vectors to define a convex cone and returns a provably minimal recipe or a Farkas/KKT certificate proving infeasibility and naming genes needing activation, with held-out-gene and null validation plus CRISPRa transfer.

method: Boolean network stable-motif/exhaustive control + multiscale ABM translation
authors: John Metzcar, Katie Pletz et al.
input_type: Boolean network model (curated logic rules) of a cell-survival network + continuous multicellular agent-based model parameters
output_type: control-set (candidate node interventions) + simulated population/spatial trajectories
limitation: relies on a curated/inferred discrete Boolean logic network rather than measured perturbation-effect vectors, uses implicit-time attractor logic with heuristic discrete-to-continuous thresholding, and provides no infeasibility proof or minimal-recipe guarantee at the multicellular scale
one_line_delta: Our oracle composes measured CRISPRi effect vectors into a convex cone and returns a falsifiable verdict—reachable with a minimal recipe or provably infeasible with a Farkas certificate naming genes to activate—whereas this method searches/simulates interventions on an inferred Boolean logic network and only forward-simulates their multicellular consequences, with no measured-effect basis, no minimal-recipe guarantee, and no formal infeasibility proof.

authors: David Murrugarra, Alan Veliz‐Cuba et al.
input_type: Boolean network model (logical update rules) of a gene regulatory network
output_type: control-set (minimal node interventions driving network to a target attractor)
limitation: depends on a hand-curated/inferred Boolean logic model rather than measured perturbation effect vectors, and offers no quantitative infeasibility proof or minimal-recipe certificate when a target is unreachable
one_line_delta: Our oracle certifies reachability directly from measured CRISPRi effect vectors via a convex cone, returning either a minimal knockdown recipe or a Farkas infeasibility certificate naming genes to activate, whereas this method finds control sets by algebraic/canalizing decomposition of a pre-specified Boolean logic model with no such measured-data-grounded feasibility guarantee.

authors: Van-Giang Trinh, Kyu Hyong Park et al.
input_type: Boolean network model (logic rules)
output_type: attractor-set + control-set (succession diagram, stable motifs)
limitation: relies on a hand-curated/inferred Boolean logic model rather than measured perturbation-effect vectors; no convex-cone feasibility proof or Farkas infeasibility certificate, and no minimal quantitative recipe
one_line_delta: biobalm analyzes a given Boolean logic model's attractor landscape and derives discrete control strategies, whereas our oracle uses measured CRISPRi effect vectors to issue a falsifiable reachable/infeasible verdict with a minimal knockdown recipe or a Farkas certificate naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Domitilla Del Vecchio
input_type: ODE/dynamical models of the biomolecular 'plant' + known perturbation/disturbance structure
output_type: control-law / circuit architecture (feedback or feedforward controller) driving a regulator to a set-point
limitation: requires an explicit mechanistic (ODE) plant model and hand-designed biomolecular controller rather than measured combinatorial perturbation data; no systematic reachability/infeasibility proof or minimal-recipe search over gene sets
one_line_delta: This paper engineers a model-based feedback/feedforward controller to hold one known regulator at a set-point, whereas our oracle uses measured multi-gene knockdown effect vectors to give a data-driven, certificate-backed verdict (reachable-with-minimal-recipe or Farkas-infeasible-with-named-activation-targets) over the whole combinatorial cell-state-shift space.

method: Computational blueprints for cell fate programming (review/framework)
authors: Pengyi Yang
input_type: scRNA-seq/spatial multi-omics + literature priors + perturbation screen data (synthesized survey, not a single algorithm)
output_type: narrative framework/pipeline recommendations + prioritized TF/small-molecule lists
limitation: relies on inferred GRNs/annotation/trajectory tools with no measured-effect convex structure, no certificate of infeasibility, no minimal recipe guarantee - it is a qualitative pipeline synthesis, not a solver
one_line_delta: This review surveys heuristic, correlation-based computational tools for prioritizing TFs/molecules within an iterative design-test-learn loop, whereas our oracle uses measured CRISPRi effects to build a convex cone yielding a provable reachable/infeasible verdict with a minimal recipe or Farkas certificate naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Matthew W. Funk, Yuhe Wang et al.
input_type: single-cell eQTL data (genotype + scRNA-seq across cell states)
output_type: cell state-specific causal gene regulatory network (eQTL-based edges)
limitation: relies on inferred/correlational GRN from eQTL associations rather than measured perturbation effects; no reachability or infeasibility guarantee, no minimal recipe
one_line_delta: Airqtl statistically infers causal regulatory edges from genotype-expression correlations across cell states, whereas our oracle uses measured CRISPRi effect vectors to issue a falsifiable reachable/infeasible verdict with a minimal knockdown recipe or a Farkas certificate naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Yuhong Huang, Chao Liu et al.
input_type: single-cell multi-omics (scRNA-seq + scATAC-seq) + ChIP-seq/HiC priors
output_type: embedding / regulatory network (TF-CRE-TG edges) + in silico perturbation trajectory prediction
limitation: relies on an inferred/learned heterogeneous GRN (attention weights, not measured causal effects), giving only forward simulation of specified perturbations with no reachability proof, no minimal recipe, and no infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: SMOGT learns a hierarchical attention-weighted network from multi-omics correlations and forward-simulates given perturbations, whereas our oracle composes measured CRISPRi effect vectors into a convex cone to return a falsifiable reachable/infeasible verdict with a minimal knockdown recipe or a Farkas certificate naming genes needing activation.

authors: Mineto Ota, Jeffrey P. Spence et al.
input_type: Perturb-seq-derived GRNs + LoF burden gene-trait effect sizes
output_type: causal graph / mechanistic pathway ranking
limitation: relies on inferred GRN structure and correlational burden-test effect sizes to build explanatory graphs, with no reachability query, no minimal perturbation recipe, and no infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: This method infers an explanatory regulator-to-trait causal graph from statistical associations, whereas our oracle uses measured knockdown effects to give a falsifiable convex-cone verdict—reachable with a minimal recipe or provably infeasible with a Farkas certificate naming genes to activate.

authors: Clelia Corridori, Merrit Romeike et al.
input_type: unperturbed scRNA-seq data (no priors)
output_type: prediction (simulated gene expression under KO/perturbation)
limitation: relies on an inferred (not measured) GRN via Ising inverse problem; purely forward simulation with no reachability/infeasibility proof or minimal-recipe guarantee
one_line_delta: IGNITE forward-simulates expression changes from a statistically inferred GRN for user-chosen knockouts, whereas our oracle uses measured perturbation effects to define a convex cone and returns a provable reachable/infeasible verdict with a minimal knockdown recipe or a Farkas certificate naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Guihai Feng, Xin Qin et al.
input_type: unperturbed bulk/single-cell transcriptomic data + pre-built high-confidence GRN priors
output_type: GRN / ranking (master TFs) / forward simulation of perturbation effects
limitation: relies on transfer-learned/inferred GRN edges (correlational, not measured knockdown effects), so predictions are unfalsifiable estimates with no proof of infeasibility or minimality
one_line_delta: CellPolaris forward-simulates perturbation outcomes on an inferred, correlational GRN with no reachability guarantee, whereas our oracle uses measured CRISPRi effect vectors in a convex cone to return a falsifiable verdict—either a minimal knockdown recipe reaching the target or a Farkas/KKT certificate proving it unreachable and naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Zhenhui He, Kangning Dong
input_type: unperturbed scRNA-seq + inferred cluster-specific GRN priors
output_type: consensus clustering + rpcUMAP embedding
limitation: relies on an inferred (not measured) GRN and simulated perturbation propagation, with no reachability proof, no minimal recipe, and no infeasibility certificate - it amplifies signal for clustering, it does not test whether any target expression shift is achievable
one_line_delta: scMagnifier forward-simulates in silico TF perturbations on an inferred GRN purely to sharpen cell-subtype clustering, whereas our oracle uses measured CRISPRi effect vectors as convex-cone generators to return a falsifiable reachable/infeasible verdict, a minimal knockdown recipe, or a Farkas certificate naming genes needing activation.

authors: Abhinav Adduri, Dhruv Gautam et al.
input_type: scRNA-seq (unperturbed + Perturb-seq, 100M+ perturbed cells, 167M observational cells)
output_type: prediction (predicted expression shift / cell embedding)
limitation: correlational transformer prediction with no minimal-recipe or infeasibility proof; forward-only, no guarantee predicted shift is actually achievable
one_line_delta: State forward-predicts expression changes for given perturbations via learned embeddings with no guarantees, whereas our oracle uses measured knockdown effects in a convex cone to return a provable reachable/unreachable verdict, minimal knockdown recipe, or Farkas infeasibility certificate naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Seowon Chang, Anna Shcherbina et al.
input_type: single/combinatorial Perturb-seq scRNA-seq + gene-gene interaction graph prior
output_type: sparse latent factor embedding + predicted expression for unseen combinations
limitation: correlational latent factorization with no reachability proof or minimal recipe; requires prior interaction graph and offers no infeasibility certificate
one_line_delta: PertSpectra forward-predicts perturbation effects via a regularized factorization embedding, whereas our oracle poses reachability as a convex-cone feasibility problem over measured knockdown effects, returning either a minimal recipe or a Farkas infeasibility certificate naming genes to activate instead.

authors: Young-Hyun Han, Hyunjin Kim et al.
